---
title: "Resources"
---

This page collects the **reading material** for the workshop --- a set of self-contained chapters covering the computational foundations and reference material participants should consult as they work through the lectures and exercises. Each chapter is a regular HTML page; read it like a short book chapter, not slides.

## Computational foundations

Background reading on the tools and concepts the workshop assumes you've seen at least once. Skim what you already know, read what you don't.

| # | Chapter | Topic |
|---|---|---|
| A | [Computer Systems & Computing Fundamentals](Resources_Folder/Appendix_A_Computing_Fundamentals.html) | How computers, operating systems, and HPC clusters work; the model that everything else builds on |
| B | [Unix & the Command Line](Resources_Folder/Appendix_B_Unix_Command_Line.html) | Navigating a Unix filesystem, common commands, pipes and redirection |
| C | [GREP, Regular Expressions & Shell Scripting](Resources_Folder/Appendix_C_GREP_Regex_Shell.html) | Pattern matching with `grep`, regex syntax, writing reusable shell scripts |
| D | [R Programming Fundamentals](Resources_Folder/Appendix_D_R_Programming.html) | R basics — data structures, functions, the tidyverse, the package ecosystem |
| E | [Git, GitHub & Version Control](Resources_Folder/Appendix_E_Git_GitHub.html) | Tracking changes with Git, collaborating on GitHub, common workflows |
| F | [High-Performance Computing with Talapas](Resources_Folder/Appendix_F_HPC_Talapas.html) | UO's HPC cluster — accounts, login, modules, jobs |

## Workshop reference

| Chapter | Topic |
|---|---|
| [Glossary](Resources_Folder/Glossary.html) | Working glossary of single-cell terminology used across the workshop |
| [VS Code & Talapas](Resources_Folder/VSCode_Talapas.html) | Setting up VS Code with the Remote --- SSH extension, plus a UO-specific overview of Talapas (login, partitions, storage, modules) |

## How to use these resources

::: callout-tip
**Suggested order if you're new to the toolchain:** A → B → C → E → D → F. Read the *Glossary* alongside the lectures; consult *VS Code & Talapas* once you have a UO account and are ready to run anything on the cluster.
:::

For curated **single-cell tutorials, papers, and external references**, see [Additional Readings](Additional_Readings.qmd).
